subject: mm/hwpoison: persist poisoned PFN list across kexec via KHO [RFC]

reviews.author: Pratyush Yadav
reviews.summary: Reviewer Pratyush Yadav suggested denying kexec if any pages in the scratch memory area are poisoned, as this would prevent unreliable booting; he also noted that an early check for poisoned pages is necessary due to recent changes
reviews.raw_body:
With KHO, you have "scratch memory", a pre-reserved area of memory on
cold boot. The kernel image is always in this area when KHO is used. I
think it would be a fair idea to deny kexec if any of the pages in this
scratch area are poisoned. Because at that point you can't reliably boot
anyway.

Normally, all allocations between setup_arch() and memblock_free_all()
_also_ happen from scratch memory, so this check would solve the first
problem too... but I recently added patches [0] to change this. So I
think we do need to identify the poisoned pages early in boot.

[0] https://lore.kernel.org/kexec/20260605183501.3884950-16-pratyush@kernel.org/

[...]

-- 
Regards,
Pratyush Yadav

reviews.author: Breno Leitao
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that fs_initcall_sync is not sufficient for prevention and recovery of memory failures due to its timing, which may miss allocations between setup_arch() and memblock_free_all(), as well as the kernel image itself, and suggested using EFI_UNUSABLE_MEMORY instead.

Reviewer Breno Leitao agreed that the patch's wording was incorrect and that running memory_failure() against an already-allocated frame is not ideal, but still better than the current situation.

Reviewer suggested using EFI_UNUSABLE_MEMORY to mark poisoned pages instead of KHO, allowing kexec to handle the issue without needing KHO.
reviews.raw_body:
Hello Kiryl, 

First of all, thanks for the review and topics raised!

On Wed, Jun 24, 2026 at 01:04:19PM +0100, Kiryl Shutsemau wrote:

---

Agreed - that wording was wrong. It is recovery, not prevention, and running
memory_failure() against an already-allocated (possibly kernel) frame is the
not ideal, but, still better than what we have today.

---

Thanks, I am wondering if we piggy-back on this EFI_UNUSABLE_MEMORY (or
something similar), than we don't need to use KHO at all, basically just marked
the page as EFI_UNUSABLE_MEMORY at poison time, and rely on kexec to avoid
passing this page forward.

Thanks for the discussion,
--breno

reviews.author: Kiryl Shutsemau (author)
reviews.summary: Author responded to Breno Leitao's concern that fs_initcall_sync is not sufficient for prevention, and instead suggested it's a recovery mechanism. Author then pushed back on the idea of adding EFI-specific code, asking if non-EFI platforms are worth caring about.
reviews.raw_body:
No. It is EFI-only thingy.

Is there non-EFI server platforms worth caring about?

-- 
  Kiryl Shutsemau / Kirill A. Shutemov

2026-06-24.patch_summary: This patch addresses a problem where poisoned PFN (Page Frame Number) lists are not persisted across kexec, which can lead to memory failures in the new kernel. The issue arises because some memory allocations occur before buddy is initialized and can land on bad frames. To solve this, the patch proposes using EFI_UNUSABLE_MEMORY to mark non-usable memory as nomap, making it invisible to both allocators and KASLR. This approach ensures that the bad frame is not accessible in the new kernel.
